title: CronAiAthon-Pipeline.psm1 triple-site code corruption destroying Add-PipelineItem function
description: CronAiAthon-Pipeline.psm1 had 3 corruption sites causing 4 parse errors at lines 207, 222, 405, 428. Site 1: Orphaned duplicate of Initialize-PipelineRegistry ending plus the ENTIRE Add-PipelineItem function definition was deleted/replaced with garbage. Site 2: $validTransitions hashtable in Test-StatusTransition lost BLOCKED/FAILED/CLOSED entries and had an orphaned try/catch registry-loading block spliced in. Site 3: Export-CentralMasterToDo try/catch body was spliced into Get-CentralMasterToDo bug tracker foreach loop, replacing null-check and property extraction. File grew from 1364 to 1434 lines (70 corrupted lines). The Add-PipelineItem function was completely absent from the module despite being listed in Export-ModuleMember.
resolution: Restored from clean backup (.history/modules/CronAiAthon-Pipeline_20260404204519.psm1) with Write-Warning to Write-AppLog conversion applied. All 3 corruption sites repaired. File now 1365 lines, 0 parse errors, all 29 functions intact including restored Add-PipelineItem.
detection_method: PSParser tokenize showed 4 errors. Backup comparison revealed 3 corruption sites and 70 extraneous lines. Corruption occurred between .history timestamps 204519 (clean) and 204719 (corrupted).
root_cause: Automated remediation tool (Write-Warning to Write-AppLog conversion) spliced code blocks incorrectly, duplicating partial function bodies and deleting the Add-PipelineItem function entirely. The tool operated on large contiguous sections and misaligned insertion points.
exception_message: ParserError at lines 207, 222, 405, 428: Unexpected token / Missing closing '}' / Missing function body
preventionRule:
1. Always run PSParser tokenize check after any automated file rewrite.
2. Verify function count before and after automated edits.
3. Use .history backup comparison to validate changes.
4. Never operate on overlapping code regions in a single pass.
5. Pin critical functions (like Add-PipelineItem) in a smoke test.
